title: The Bithumb–Binance BTC-numéraire Premium: A Theoretical Framework with Calibrated Simulation Evidence on BNB Chain Tokens (Trilingual Edition)
description:
This paper redefines the price discrepancy between the Korean exchange Bithumb and the global exchange Binance using a new measure of the kimchi premium expressed in BTC units, denoted πBTCX(t), accompanied by calibrated simulation evidence on daily series over 2020–2025.

The BTC-numéraire premium is invariant to exchange-rate and stablecoin-level fluctuations by numerator–denominator cancellation (Proposition 1). A normalised variance ratio ρX controls for token-level own volatility, OLS regressions are reported with Newey–West HAC standard errors, and asymmetric volatility is estimated with an EGARCH(1,1) model using Student-t innovations. A cross-exchange comparison among Binance, OKX, Bybit and Bithumb identifies the kimchi premium as a regulatory cost rather than a market inefficiency, and KCS-detected illegal forex transactions (approximately 14.2 trillion KRW over 2018–2025, of which 2.04 trillion KRW in 2024–2025) are reported as lower-bound indicators of the policy cost.

Available in Korean (한국어), English, and Chinese (中文) editions. The release includes the full LaTeX sources, compiled PDFs, all 11 figures (English-labeled, shared across editions), 15 data CSVs, and reproduction scripts (Python + docx-js).

subjects.term: G14 General Financial Markets: Information and Market Efficiency
G18 Financial Markets: Government Policy and Regulation

notes: Three language editions (Korean / English / Chinese) included in the release. Empirical results are based on synthetic data calibrated to published stylized facts; precise parameter values must be re-estimated on real exchange data by follow-up empirical work.
